sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर। असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः॥
transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṃ kāryaṃ karma samācara / asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ
literal_meaning: Therefore, without attachment, constantly perform action as duty; for, performing action without attachment, a person attains the Supreme.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes duty as an ongoing, non-attached engagement that dissolves egoic reward-seeking and aligns the psyche with a trans-personal order, thereby reaching the highest psychological integration.
psychological_state: Purposeful engagement characterized by low neuroticism, high conscientiousness, and intrinsic motivation; resembles Csikszentmihalyi’s flow minus ego-reward.
trigger_scenario: A project manager must ship a critical release; instead of obsessing over quarterly bonus or blame, she channels full attention into the task because the product helps thousands of users—success or failure does not define her worth.
anchor_text: Do your duty steadily, letting go of outcomes.
